symptom: Retention times shifted earlier than expected
likely_causes: column degradation
mobile phase composition change
temperature fluctuation
diagnostics: check column age and void volume
verify mobile phase batch
check column oven temperature log
corrective_actions: replace column
prepare fresh mobile phase
recalibrate column oven
escalation_conditions: shift > 10% of expected RT
multiple analytes affected simultaneously

symptom: Gradual loss of sensitivity over multiple injections
likely_causes: matrix buildup on source
dirty spray shield
blocked capillary
diagnostics: inspect spray shield visually
run solvent blank and compare background
check capillary condition
corrective_actions: clean source components
replace spray shield
flush capillary with strong solvent
escalation_conditions: sensitivity loss > 50%
source cleaning does not restore signal

symptom: Unexpected peaks appearing in blank injections
likely_causes: contaminated inlet liner
septum bleed
column bleed
solvent impurity
diagnostics: run solvent blank
bake out column at high temperature
replace inlet liner and septum
corrective_actions: replace liner and septum
bake out column
use higher purity solvent
escalation_conditions: ghost peaks co-elute with target analytes
persist after liner replacement

symptom: Noisy or drifting mass signal during TGA measurement
likely_causes: external vibration from HVAC, foot traffic, or nearby equipment
air drafts reaching the balance chamber
static electricity on sample or ceramic crucible
contaminated or mechanically damaged hang-down wire
sample mass exceeding balance capacity (>1 g)
diagnostics: run an empty-crucible baseline scan and quantify baseline noise level
verify purge gas flow rate (typically 40–60 mL/min N2 balance + 60 mL/min furnace)
inspect hang-down wire under magnification for contamination, kinks, or corrosion
temporarily close off lab HVAC and check if noise improves
verify instrument is on a vibration-isolated bench or anti-vibration pad
corrective_actions: relocate instrument away from vibration sources; use vibration-isolation pad
shield instrument from air drafts; maintain stable purge gas flow rate
clean hang-down wire with isopropanol-wetted lint-free swab; allow to dry fully
reduce sample mass to 5–20 mg for optimal signal-to-noise
replace hang-down wire if corrosion or mechanical damage is confirmed
escalation_conditions: noise exceeds 0.01 mg RMS after all corrective actions
hang-down wire visibly broken, fused, or severely corroded
balance drift > 0.5 mg/min on empty crucible baseline

symptom: Onset or decomposition temperature does not match expected literature or previous results
likely_causes: temperature sensor drift requiring recalibration
heating rate too fast causing thermal lag between furnace and sample
temperature calibration not performed with certified Curie-point standards
sample mass too large (> 20 mg) introducing internal thermal gradients
furnace atmosphere (oxidising vs inert) not matching literature conditions
diagnostics: run Curie-point temperature calibration using certified standards (e.g., Alumel ~154°C, Nickel ~358°C, Iron ~780°C)
compare onset temperature at multiple heating rates (5, 10, 20 °C/min) to extrapolate to zero-rate
verify purge gas identity and flow rate match conditions of reference measurement
repeat measurement with 5 mg sample and compare onset with previous 20 mg run
review temperature calibration date in TA Instruments TRIOS software
corrective_actions: perform full temperature calibration using TA Instruments Curie-point standards kit
use slower heating rate (5–10 °C/min) for precise onset temperature determination
reduce sample mass to ≤ 10 mg to minimise thermal gradients
match purge gas and flow rate exactly to the published reference conditions
contact TA Instruments service if calibration does not hold between consecutive runs
escalation_conditions: temperature offset > 5°C after recalibration
Curie-point transitions no longer sharp (broad over > 10°C range)
furnace thermocouple visibly damaged or oxidised

symptom: Apparent mass gain at the beginning of heating or a systematic upward drift in mass with increasing temperature on an empty crucible
likely_causes: buoyancy effect: gas density decreases with rising temperature, reducing buoyant force on crucible
no baseline subtraction applied to sample run
using high-density inert gas (Argon) without buoyancy correction
gas flow rate changed between baseline and sample run
diagnostics: run an empty crucible baseline under identical conditions (same gas, flow, temperature programme)
check if apparent mass gain magnitude scales with gas density (Ar > N2 > He)
overlay sample curve with empty-crucible baseline to isolate true mass loss
verify gas flow rate is identical between baseline and sample measurement
corrective_actions: subtract empty-crucible baseline from sample data in TRIOS software
enable automatic buoyancy correction in TRIOS method settings
use helium purge if lowest buoyancy artifact is required
run baseline and sample at the same gas, flow rate, and temperature programme without exception
escalation_conditions: apparent mass gain > 0.5 mg on empty crucible after gas stabilisation
artifact prevents accurate mass loss determination for small events (< 1% mass loss)

symptom: Sample shows exothermic mass gain or oxidative decomposition under nominally inert (N2 or Ar) purge
likely_causes: insufficient purge time before starting ramp (residual O2 in furnace)
O2 leak through gas line fittings, tubing cracks, or loose connections
gas purity insufficient (< 99.999% N2 or Ar)
ambient air back-diffusion through furnace exhaust port
wrong gas cylinder connected to balance or furnace purge line
diagnostics: increase pre-run purge time to ≥ 15 minutes at high flow (100 mL/min) before starting
verify gas purity certificate; use only 99.999% (5.0 grade) N2 or Ar
inspect all gas line fittings with soapy water or leak detector spray for bubbles
run a blank with easily oxidised reference material (e.g., pure carbon black) to confirm inertness
check outlet of furnace for back-flow by holding a tissue near exhaust
corrective_actions: extend pre-run purge time to ≥ 15 minutes; use an oxygen scavenger on the gas line
replace gas tubing and tighten all Swagelok fittings; leak-test under pressure
switch to certified 99.999% inert gas cylinder from reputable supplier
install an O2 trap/purifier inline between the cylinder and instrument
add a positive pressure relief port to prevent air back-diffusion at exhaust
escalation_conditions: O2 contamination confirmed but source cannot be identified after full line inspection
furnace ceramic or thermocouple shows signs of oxidative damage
sample safety hazard due to unexpected combustion

symptom: Mass loss percentage varies significantly between replicate runs of the same material
likely_causes: sample inhomogeneity (particle size, mixing)
variable sample packing density in crucible
moisture or solvent uptake between sample preparation and loading
platinum or alumina crucible contamination from previous run
inconsistent sample mass between replicates
diagnostics: compare mass loss curves normalised to sample mass — are onset temperatures consistent?
check if variance correlates with sample particle size or preparation method
measure sample immediately after weighing to minimise moisture uptake
inspect crucibles for residue; run empty crucible through full temperature programme
record exact sample mass for each replicate and compare normalised curves
corrective_actions: grind and sieve sample to a uniform particle size (< 100 µm) before analysis
use consistent tamping/packing pressure when loading crucible
store prepared samples in a desiccator and analyse within 30 minutes of preparation
anneal platinum crucibles at 900°C for 30 min after each use to burn off residues
target the same sample mass (± 0.5 mg) for all replicates using a 5-place balance
escalation_conditions: RSD of mass loss % exceeds 5% across ≥ 5 replicates after addressing all corrective actions
onset temperature varies by > 10°C between replicates of the same sample

symptom: High-frequency noise or erratic fluctuations on DSC heat-flow baseline
likely_causes: electrical interference from power supply or nearby equipment
vibration transmitted to cell sensors
purge gas flow too high causing turbulence inside cell
loose or corroded electrical connections at cell
condensation inside DSC cell (sub-ambient measurements)
diagnostics: run an empty-cell baseline at the same conditions and quantify baseline noise (µW RMS)
compare noise at different scan rates (1, 5, 10, 20 °C/min) — thermal noise scales with rate
temporarily unplug nearby instruments and check if noise improves
inspect all cable connections from cell to electronic module
verify purge gas (N2 or He) flow rate is 50 mL/min or as specified in method
corrective_actions: connect instrument to a dedicated power circuit with line conditioner
check and firmly seat all cell electrical connections; clean with contact spray if corroded
set purge gas flow to 50 mL/min N2 or 25 mL/min He; avoid turbulent flow
for sub-ambient work: dry cell with dry N2 purge overnight; use dry gas generator
use lower heating rate to reduce inherent noise floor
escalation_conditions: baseline noise > 10 µW RMS at 10 °C/min after all corrective actions
cell sensor reads implausible temperature (> 10°C from setpoint at equilibrium)
visible moisture or contamination inside the DSC cell

symptom: Glass transition temperature (Tg) is lower or higher than expected or not reproducible between runs
likely_causes: different heating rate from literature reference (Tg shifts ~3–5°C per decade of heating rate)
thermal history not erased: residual stress or crystallinity from processing
residual moisture plasticising the polymer (lowers Tg)
temperature calibration offset (instrument Tg ≠ true Tg)
sample thickness too great causing thermal lag
diagnostics: perform heat-cool-heat cycle: first heat erases history, Tg from second heat is intrinsic
check and match heating rate exactly to the reference publication
dry sample in oven at T_onset - 30°C for 2h and re-measure immediately
verify temperature calibration using indium (156.6°C onset) and cyclohexane (-87°C) or other certified standard
reduce sample mass to ≤ 5 mg to minimise thermal lag
corrective_actions: always report Tg from second heating scan after controlled cooling at 10 °C/min
dry hygroscopic samples at 60–80°C in vacuum for ≥ 2h before DSC
recalibrate temperature axis using indium; use MDSC for weak Tg signals
standardise heating rate across all runs and always quote rate when reporting Tg
crimp hermetic pans to prevent moisture re-absorption between preparation and measurement
escalation_conditions: Tg variation > 5°C between replicates using the same thermal history protocol
indium calibration onset temperature deviates > 1°C from 156.6°C

symptom: Melting or crystallisation peak is unusually broad, split, or poorly resolved from baseline
likely_causes: poor thermal contact between sample and pan bottom (uneven sample distribution)
heating rate too fast for sample thermal mass
sample particle size too large causing internal temperature gradients
hermetic pan lid not crimped flat, creating air gap
sample contains a wide distribution of crystal sizes or polymorphs
diagnostics: press sample flat to ensure intimate pan contact; use spatula or press tool before crimping
repeat measurement at 2 and 5 °C/min and compare peak width — broad peaks narrow at slow rate
check lid crimp: a properly crimped pan has no gap visible around the edge
compare peak shape with known reference material (e.g., indium) to rule out instrument artefact
cross-section the sample pan after measurement and inspect sample distribution
corrective_actions: press powdered sample flat in pan with a micro-spatula before crimping lid
reduce heating rate to 2–5 °C/min for materials with high thermal mass (e.g., metals, ceramics)
reduce sample mass to 2–5 mg and increase sample surface-to-mass ratio
use TA Instruments hermetic pan crimping tool; inspect seal under microscope
for polymorphic samples: apply a standard annealing pre-treatment and document
escalation_conditions: peak width at half-height is > 5× broader than indium reference under same conditions
split peak cannot be narrowed by reducing sample mass or heating rate (suggests true polymorphism)

symptom: Calculated enthalpy (ΔH) values are inconsistent between replicates or deviate significantly from literature
likely_causes: enthalpy calibration not performed or performed with incorrect indium mass
pan mass mismatch between sample and reference pans (> 0.5 mg difference)
incorrect baseline integration limits applied in TRIOS software
sample reacts with pan material (e.g., corrosive samples in aluminium pans)
incomplete transition: sample not fully melted or crystallised within scan range
diagnostics: run indium calibration standard (> 3 mg, known literature ΔHf = 28.57 J/g) and check recovery
weigh sample and reference pans on a 5-place balance; pair pans within ± 0.3 mg
review integration baseline selection in TRIOS — use sigmoid baseline for overlapping transitions
check if sample reacts with pan (discolouration, pitting, or gas evolution) after run
extend temperature range to ensure complete transition before integration
corrective_actions: perform indium enthalpy calibration with fresh certified standard (minimum 3 mg accurately weighed)
use weight-matched sample and reference pans; pre-weigh and sort pans in batches
apply consistent baseline type (linear or sigmoid) across all replicates; document in SOP
switch to gold, platinum, or stainless-steel pans for reactive or corrosive samples
widen scan range by ±20°C beyond expected transition to capture full peak area
escalation_conditions: indium ΔH recovery < 95% or > 105% of 28.57 J/g after recalibration
enthalpy RSD > 5% across ≥ 5 replicates of same material and preparation

symptom: DSC baseline is curved, non-linear, or returns to a different level after a transition
likely_causes: scan rate too high for instrument thermal equilibration
asymmetric loading: sample and reference pans have different heat capacity or contact quality
cell contamination from previous run
pan contact problem — pan not sitting flat on sensor
heat capacity of sample changes significantly over the scanned range (common in polymers)
diagnostics: run empty-cell baseline and empty matched-pan baseline; overlay with sample run
check if curvature direction reverses when empty pans are swapped between sample and reference positions
inspect DSC cell for residue; clean with dry cotton swab if contaminated
reduce scan rate to 10 °C/min and re-run; excessive curvature at high rates suggests thermal asymmetry
in TRIOS, subtract the matched empty-pan run from the sample run as instrument baseline correction
corrective_actions: limit scan rate to ≤ 20 °C/min for standard measurements; use 10 °C/min for best baseline
use weight-matched pans in both sample and reference positions
clean DSC cell sensors with dry, lint-free cotton swab; use isopropanol only for persistent residue
ensure pans sit flat with no rocking — press gently with flat tool after placement
apply matched empty-pan baseline subtraction in TRIOS data analysis for precision work
escalation_conditions: baseline step after empty-pan subtraction > 1 mW at any scan rate ≤ 10 °C/min
residue in cell does not respond to routine cleaning (requires field service)
